title: Decoder output-projection sharded weight-store interface contract
summary: Validate a bounded RTL/perf-sim ready/valid contract for the sharded resident weight-store interface implied by the output-projection weight-store feasibility result.
motivation: The resident output-projection weight-store feasibility result selected large bank counts and high aggregate read widths.
Those values should not be naively instantiated as a monolithic register or SRAM block in the next RTL job.

Before measuring producer-side logic, the sharded read interface needs an equivalence check against a perf-sim model so later producer integration preserves request/response ordering and data selection behavior.

requested_items.objective: Probe a bounded RTL/perf-sim sharded ready/valid weight-store interface derived from the resident output-projection weight-store feasibility rows.

expected_result.reason: If the bounded sharded interface matches perf-sim, use the contract for a measured producer-side weight-fetch wrapper while keeping full storage PPA from the feasibility model.

success_criteria: The probe reads the merged weight-store feasibility artifact.
The Python perf reference and RTL simulation agree on accepted requests, response count, and deterministic checksum for representative sharded-bank cases.
The report clearly states that full resident storage capacity and area remain proxy-modeled rather than synthesized.
non_goals: Do not instantiate the full 37-bank or 50-bank resident store as flip-flops.
Do not claim SRAM macro PPA from this bounded interface-contract job.
Do not replace the producer or ranker equivalence contracts.
